ELPAC Level 3
Lexile ≥1400L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Fox Shaped Hole

STS-MS7-L3-1400P-MSSCI7-PZQ-MNA-02 • MS Science Grade 7 • Patterns of Interactions Among Organisms
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
At the oasis field station, every relationship is a puzzle piece, and the wall is not finished until each pair of living things is matched to the way they affect each other. In the oases of North Africa and the Middle East, date palms have shaded and fed people for thousands of years, and the bees that visit their flowers get food while the palms get pollen carried from tree to tree. Fennec foxes hunt jerboas across the same sand at night, and gazelles crowd one small water hole when the dry season comes.
If the bees disappeared from this oasis, what do you think would happen to the date palms, and to the people who depend on them?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Yara doing?
Look at the picture. Explain what Yara is doing and why it matters to the group's work. Connect her task to what the others are doing.
2
What is Karim holding?
Look at the picture. Explain what Karim is holding and why that animal belongs in this activity. Use the word because.
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Explain what the teacher is doing and how her action completes the group's thinking. Use the words so that.
4
What place is this? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Explain what place this is and how you know, then explain why this place suits the work these students are doing.
5
Tell me about this wall.
Look at the picture. Explain how the big wall is organized and what its sections tell you about how living things affect each other.
6
Tell me about the clothes Salma is wearing.
Look at the picture. Explain what Salma is wearing and why field clothing matters when students work outdoors.
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: NGSS MS-LS2-2 — Patterns of Interactions Among Organisms (competition, predation, mutualism). ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
